headline: A credential-theft campaign that pried open some 75,000 Fortinet firewalls now feeds the INC and Lynx ransomware gangs, while an 81-million-attempt password spray batters Azure CLI and vendors rush out maximum-severity patches.

emerging_trends.text: Stolen credentials increasingly serve as the on-ramp to ransomware, with the FortiBleed haul from roughly 75,000 Fortinet firewalls now steering victims toward the INC and Lynx crews.
emerging_trends.topic_title: FortiBleed Fuels Lynx and INC Ransomware

emerging_trends.text: Frontier models cut both ways this week as attackers registered AI-hallucinated web addresses through phantom squatting and coaxed DeepSeek into writing functional browser ransomware.
emerging_trends.topic_title: AI Turns Attacker: Phantom Domains, Browser Ransomware, Cursor Escapes

emerging_trends.text: Defenders faced a two-front vulnerability crunch, patching seven CVSS 10.0 Adobe ColdFusion flaws and Citrix's HTTP/2 Bomb even as fresh exploitation battered SharePoint and Oracle E-Business Suite.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Adobe, Citrix, Apple, and Google Ship Critical Fixes

topics.title: ClickFix Becomes the Default Malware Lure
topics.latest_developments: Dark Reading declared ClickFix the dominant malware-delivery technique across current attacks, as separate research found phishing kits fingerprinting a victim's user-agent data to serve OS-specific payloads, Securonix detailed the VEIL#DROP chain dropping the PureLogs stealer through Blogger pages, and BleepingComputer flagged ChocoPoC, a Python remote-access trojan hidden in trojanized GitHub proof-of-concept exploits aimed at security researchers.
topics.summary: Social-engineering delivery keeps evolving, with ClickFix's fake human-verification prompts now the norm and campaigns tailoring payloads to each victim's device. Defenders should block manual command-paste tricks, scrutinize proof-of-concept code, and train staff on the ClickFix pattern.

sources.title: And the Winner in Dominant Malware Delivery? ClickFix
Crafty Phishing Campaigns Auto-Adapt to Victim's Device, OS
VEIL#DROP Malware Chain Uses Blogger Platform to Deliver PureLogs Stealer
New ChocoPoC malware targets researchers via trojanized PoC exploits

topics.title: Adobe, Citrix, Apple, and Google Ship Critical Fixes
topics.latest_developments: Adobe patched seven maximum-severity CVSS 10.0 flaws in ColdFusion and Campaign Classic on July 1, Citrix fixed six NetScaler bugs including the new HTTP/2 Bomb and a CitrixBleed-style information-disclosure flaw, Apple issued dozens of iOS, macOS, and Safari fixes, and Google closed 382 Chrome vulnerabilities.
topics.summary: Four major vendors released critical updates in one day, the Adobe and Citrix flaws opening paths to code execution, file reads, and denial of service. Administrators should apply the ColdFusion, NetScaler, Apple, and Chrome patches immediately.

sources.title: Adobe Patches Critical ColdFusion, Campaign Classic Vulnerabilities
Citrix Patches NetScaler Vulnerabilities, Including New 'HTTP/2 Bomb' Attack
Apple Patches Dozens of Vulnerabilities Across iOS, macOS, and Safari
Google Patches 382 Chrome Vulnerabilities

topics.title: AI Turns Attacker: Phantom Domains, Browser Ransomware, Cursor Escapes
topics.latest_developments: Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 documented phantom squatting, where attackers register the web addresses large language models hallucinate and host phishing pages to catch misdirected traffic; researchers used DeepSeek to build working browser ransomware abusing the Chromium API on Windows and Android; and Cato AI Labs disclosed DuneSlide, two 9.8-rated Cursor flaws, CVE-2026-50548 and CVE-2026-50549, that let a single prompt escape the AI editor's sandbox and run commands.
topics.summary: Three separate findings show frontier AI powering offensive operations, from squatting on invented domains to generating novel ransomware and hijacking a developer's machine through prompt injection. Enterprises should watch for AI-suggested domains, restrict Cursor's command execution, and treat AI-generated code as untrusted.

sources.title: Phantom Squatting Uses AI-Hallucinated Domains for Phishing and Malware
AI-Generated Browser Ransomware Abuses Chromium API on Windows and Android
Critical Cursor Flaws Could Let Prompt Injection Escape Sandbox and Run Commands
'Phantom Squatting': An Emerging AI-Driven Supply Chain Threat

topics.title: Azure CLI Password-Spray Blitz Hits Microsoft 365
topics.latest_developments: Huntress reported an automated password-spray campaign that fired more than 81 million login attempts at Microsoft's Azure command-line interface between June 12 and June 26, compromising at least 78 accounts, all traced to an IPv6 range LSHIY LLC controls under AS32167.
topics.summary: Attackers hammered Azure CLI and Microsoft 365 logins from LSHIY infrastructure in the range 2a0a:d683::/32, breaking into dozens of accounts. Administrators should enforce multi-factor authentication and block the offending address range.

sources.title: Azure CLI Password Spray Hits at Least 78 Microsoft Accounts in 81M+ Attempts
Massive Password Spray Campaign Targeting Azure CLI
Hackers target Microsoft 365 accounts with 81 million login attempts

topics.title: Fresh Exploitation Hits SharePoint, LoadMaster, and Oracle EBS
topics.latest_developments: CISA added Microsoft SharePoint Server deserialization flaw CVE-2026-45659 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on July 1, eSentire's Threat Response Unit flagged active exploitation attempts against Progress Kemp LoadMaster command-injection flaw CVE-2026-8037, and BleepingComputer counted over 900 exposed Oracle E-Business Suite instances under attack through CVE-2026-46817.
topics.summary: Attackers are actively hitting three enterprise products, with CISA's SharePoint listing binding federal agencies to patch and roughly 900 Oracle E-Business Suite servers left exposed online. Teams should patch all three and check for web shells and rogue accounts.

sources.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
Progress Kemp LoadMaster Pre-Auth RCE Flaw Faces Active Exploitation Attempts
Over 900 Oracle E-Business instances exposed to ongoing attacks

topics.title: FortiBleed Fuels Lynx and INC Ransomware
topics.latest_developments: BleepingComputer tied the FortiBleed campaign to the INC and Lynx ransomware operations on July 1, indicating the stolen Fortinet credentials will seed future network intrusions.
topics.summary: FortiBleed pried open roughly 75,000 Fortinet firewalls and harvested credentials that the INC and Lynx gangs will likely use to break into corporate networks. Organizations running Fortinet gear should rotate credentials, audit access, and expect fallout for years.

sources.title: FortiBleed credential-theft campaign linked to Lynx ransomware
Smashing Security podcast #474: Polymarket can predict the future. So how did it miss this hack?
